observable_contract: A matching occupied bucket is returned or search stops at an EMPTY control byte.
reference_representation: Separate payload array plus replicated control-byte tail; FULL carries a seven-bit hash tag.
live_state: Only a FULL control byte authorizes equality access to its corresponding payload.
owner_traffic: No payload owner moves; hash and equality callbacks consume only borrowed inputs.
provenance: The returned bucket and any derived reference must remain tied to the same table root and mutation version.
failure_cleanup: Callback abort ends the operation; table representation and owners remain unchanged.
fact_behavior: Group matches are candidates; equality confirms a hit; EMPTY terminates the probe; mutation invalidates all such facts.
c_route: C-4 sparse lookup composed with C-7 returned-element provenance and C-11 occupancy/probe facts.
c0_dependency: C0-10 portable group/SIMD load, match, and scalar fallback row is unenumerated.
reference_events: Group loads, tag comparisons, equality only on matching FULL buckets, probe advance, and EMPTY termination; no allocation, payload move, or atomic event.
forced_delta: No structural tax is identified, but exact target-row semantics are absent.
falsifier: Enumerate one portable group row and show the existing probe performs no extra load, check, scan, or payload access.

observable_contract: A matching tag permits equality to inspect exactly the corresponding live payload.
reference_representation: Control metadata and payload reside in one allocation but in distinct regions.
live_state: The control-to-payload relation must prove the bucket live before forming a payload reference.
provenance: Equality and the returned bucket derive authority from the exact FULL slot, not from the hash or integer index.
failure_cleanup: Callback abort preserves all control bytes, counters, and payload owners.
fact_behavior: The FULL observation needs a version-bounded occupancy fact synchronized with payload liveness.
c_route: C-4 plus C-7 and C-11 are the named families, but their exact cross-family admission rule is absent.
reference_events: One metadata observation gates one payload access; no initialization, copy, allocation, indirection, scan beyond probing, atomic, or machine event is added.
forced_delta: No forced structural delta is identified; authority is unresolved.
falsifier: Define a finite same-root same-version control-to-payload rule that rejects EMPTY and DELETED without recognizing Hashbrown.

observable_contract: An EMPTY byte proves the searched key is absent along the current probe sequence.
reference_representation: Replicated control tail permits unaligned group reads across the logical end.
live_state: No payload becomes live or dead.
provenance: The miss result is tied to the table root, capacity, probe position, and mutation version.
failure_cleanup: Callback abort or ordinary miss leaves the table unchanged.
fact_behavior: EMPTY termination must be a closed probe fact and must be invalidated by insertion, removal, or rehash.
c_route: C-4 sparse search composed with C-11 probe facts; exact fact schema is absent.
reference_events: Existing group probe and EMPTY test only; no whole-table scan or payload traffic.
forced_delta: No forced structural delta is identified; the fact producer and invalidators are unspecified.
falsifier: Enumerate the probe-termination fact and show every mutation that can change reachability invalidates it.

observable_contract: Search returns a vacant index that remains usable only until table mutation.
reference_representation: An EMPTY or DELETED control slot corresponds to uninitialized payload storage.
live_state: The selected payload place remains vacant until the insertion commit.
owner_traffic: The offered key and value remain owned by the caller-side operation until committed.
provenance: The vacant index needs a same-root version token; an integer index alone grants no access.
failure_cleanup: Hash or equality abort preserves the original table and returns or destroys offered owners according to the call boundary.
fact_behavior: The vacancy fact is invalidated by any intervening mutation, resize, or rehash.
c_route: C-4 vacant-entry protocol composed with C-7 entry provenance and C-11 vacancy facts.
reference_events: Probe and vacancy selection only; no payload initialization, copy, allocation, or atomic event.
forced_delta: No forced structural delta is identified; the versioned entry token is unspecified.
falsifier: Define a non-escapable vacant token that cannot be reused after mutation and adds no runtime metadata.

observable_contract: Without growth, one vacant slot becomes FULL and receives exactly one key-value owner pair.
reference_representation: Control byte is written with the hash tag and the uninitialized payload place is then written.
live_state: The transition must prevent access in the interval between metadata commit and payload write and finish with exactly one live payload.
owner_traffic: Each offered owner moves exactly once into the selected place; no previous payload owner exists.
provenance: The resulting mutable value borrow derives from the newly live slot and table root.
failure_cleanup: No recoverable failure occurs after slot preparation in the reference path; abandonment must either precede commit or perform fixed rollback/cleanup.
fact_behavior: Control, items, growth_left, payload liveness, and all vacancy/probe facts change in one logical transition.
c_route: C-4 sparse insertion needs a fixed C-6 transition and C-7 result rule; the exact state machine is absent.
reference_events: One control update, one payload write, items increment, and growth_left decrement only for EMPTY; no zeroing or payload copy.
forced_delta: No forced tax is identified; exact sequencing, abandonment, and fact synchronization are unresolved.
falsifier: Enumerate a checked commit protocol matching the reference writes without a temporary tag, rollback scan, or extra payload move.

observable_contract: VacantEntry insertion returns a mutable reference to the inserted value.
reference_representation: The entry stores the key, table reference, hash, and vacant index; payload remains in the table allocation.
live_state: The selected slot changes from vacant to live exactly once.
owner_traffic: The stored key and supplied value move into the table; the returned reference owns nothing.
provenance: The result must derive from the inserted value field through the table root, not from the temporary entry object.
failure_cleanup: If insertion cannot commit, both offered owners require exact return or destruction; successful entry consumption needs no later cleanup.
fact_behavior: Successful insertion invalidates the vacant token and produces a version-bounded live-value fact.
c_route: C-4 plus C-6 insertion composed with C-7 entry-result provenance; exact composition is absent.
reference_events: No extra allocation or indirection beyond the reference table; one key-value write and one returned borrow.
forced_delta: No forced structural delta is identified; result provenance and invalidation are unspecified.
falsifier: Define the result-source map and show consuming the entry cannot leave an alias or cleanup obligation.

observable_contract: When growth is required, insertion acquires a larger root and then commits the item.
reference_representation: A new allocation contains payload storage, control bytes, and the replicated control tail.
live_state: Existing live slots remain authoritative until resize commit; the new selected slot becomes live after migration.
owner_traffic: Existing owners relocate; the offered pair moves once into its final vacant slot.
provenance: All resulting access must re-root to the new allocation and invalidate old-root entries.
failure_cleanup: Allocation failure must leave the old table and offered owners in their declared states.
fact_behavior: All old-root occupancy and probe facts invalidate at resize commit.
c_route: C-4/C-6 route the table protocol, but physical-root acquisition requires an exact C0 row.
c0_dependency: C0-7 growth allocation, alignment, layout, failure, and transfer row is unenumerated.
reference_events: One reference growth allocation plus reference relocation and release; no zeroing requirement is observed.
forced_delta: No extra allocation is identified; the exact permitted allocation event and failure contract are absent.
falsifier: Enumerate the selected allocation row and show failure preserves owners while success adds no second root event or zero fill.

observable_contract: Inserting an existing key retains the stored key, replaces the stored value, returns the old value, and consumes the offered key.
reference_representation: The existing FULL slot remains occupied with the same key and a new value.
live_state: Occupancy is continuous while the value owner changes.
owner_traffic: New value moves once into the slot; old value moves once to the result; offered duplicate key is destroyed once.
provenance: The returned old value is owned; any mutable reference to the new value derives from the same live slot and new mutation version.
failure_cleanup: Equality abort leaves both table owners unchanged; after match, replacement requires exact disposition for all three owners.
fact_behavior: Occupancy may remain valid, but value-content and borrow facts invalidate at replacement.
c_route: C-4 replacement needs a fixed C-6 owner-disposition transition and C-11 selective invalidators; it is unenumerated.
reference_events: One value replacement and one old-value return; no key replacement, allocation, zeroing, scan, or atomic event.
forced_delta: No forced structural delta is identified; exact owner and fact transition is absent.
falsifier: Enumerate replacement so the stored key is never moved and each offered or displaced owner has one disposition.

observable_contract: A replace-with callback may restore a replacement item or remove the slot after temporarily taking the owner.
reference_representation: One FULL slot is logically in transition; control may be restored or changed to vacant.
live_state: The original item is live in the callback input, not simultaneously in the slot.
owner_traffic: Original owner moves to the callback; returned Some owner moves back, while None leaves the slot vacant.
provenance: No reference to the temporarily vacant payload may escape; returned entry provenance depends on the branch.
failure_cleanup: Callback abort and both normal branches require exact cleanup and a declared valid table state.
fact_behavior: All payload and occupancy facts invalidate during the transition; the Some branch may produce fresh FULL facts.
c_route: C-4 needs one fixed C-6 replace/remove state machine and C-7 branch-result provenance; none is enumerated.
reference_events: At most one owner take and one owner restore; no allocation, zeroing, or unrelated metadata.
forced_delta: No forced structural delta is identified; callback-abort cleanup is unresolved.
falsifier: Define the fixed two-branch protocol and its abort cleanup without copying the item or adding a persistent tag.

observable_contract: A found FULL slot becomes EMPTY or DELETED while its key-value owner is returned.
reference_representation: Control metadata preserves probe reachability after the payload becomes vacant.
live_state: The payload is read exactly once as an owner and is no longer live after removal.
owner_traffic: The key-value owner moves once from the slot to the result; it is not destroyed in place.
provenance: The result is an owned value; the removal token must have been derived from the matching FULL slot.
failure_cleanup: A miss changes nothing; found removal has no recoverable failure after ownership transfer in the reference path.
fact_behavior: The removed slot and affected probe facts invalidate; items decrements and growth_left may change.
c_route: C-4 removal requires an exact C-6 transition and C-11 invalidation rule; the state machine is absent.
reference_events: One owner read, one control update, items decrement; no allocation, zeroing, payload copy, or atomic event.
forced_delta: No forced structural delta is identified; transition authority is unresolved.
falsifier: Enumerate removal so metadata cannot authorize a payload after the owner read and the result is disposed exactly once.

observable_contract: The control byte becomes DELETED when EMPTY would break an existing probe chain, otherwise EMPTY restores growth capacity.
reference_representation: A group-local emptiness test selects the tombstone state.
live_state: The removed payload remains vacant in either control state.
owner_traffic: No additional owner traffic beyond the removed owner.
provenance: No payload provenance is derived from EMPTY or DELETED.
failure_cleanup: No separate failure path; the table must remain searchable under either branch.
fact_behavior: DELETED preserves reachability; EMPTY permits growth_left increment; both invalidate the old FULL fact.
c_route: C-4 has the right sparse representation family, but its exact deletion/probe-maintenance relation with C-11 is absent.
c0_dependency: C0-10 group operations are also implicated, but the primary missing authority is the composition rule.
reference_events: Two group loads and control update as in the reference; no whole-table scan or persistent extra metadata.
forced_delta: No forced structural delta is identified; the branch proof and fact update are unspecified.
falsifier: Define the finite EMPTY-versus-DELETED rule and show it preserves lookup without an extra scan or metadata bit.

observable_contract: Removing an occupied entry returns the exact stored key and value pair and consumes the entry guard.
reference_representation: The guard identifies one FULL table slot.
live_state: The slot becomes vacant and cannot be accessed through the consumed guard.
owner_traffic: Both stored owners move once to the result.
provenance: The guard-to-slot relation must be same-root and version-bounded; the result is owned.
failure_cleanup: Guard consumption closes all cleanup; a miss is represented before creating the occupied guard.
fact_behavior: Mutation invalidates the guard and all slot borrows.
c_route: C-4 plus C-7 occupied-entry provenance and invalidation are named but not enumerated.
reference_events: No extra allocation, indirection, copy, zeroing, scan, or atomic event.
forced_delta: No forced structural delta is identified; guard consumption and invalidation are unspecified.
falsifier: Define the occupied-entry source map and prove no reference derived from the guard survives removal.

observable_contract: Reserve chooses in-place tombstone cleanup or resize according to capacity and growth state.
reference_representation: The existing root is reused for in-place rehash or replaced by one newly allocated root.
live_state: All live payloads remain accounted for across the selected branch.
owner_traffic: No payload owner is duplicated; branch selection itself moves none.
provenance: All entry, bucket, and probe results are invalidated by either rehash branch.
failure_cleanup: Allocation failure is possible only on resize and must preserve the old table.
fact_behavior: Branch selection invalidates old probe facts at the branch commit point.
c_route: C-4/C-6 need a closed branch and commit algebra; the exact rule is absent.
c0_dependency: C0-7 is required only for the resize branch.
reference_events: Reference conditional branch; no extra scan, allocation, or metadata beyond the chosen reference algorithm.
forced_delta: No forced structural delta is identified; branch-specific commit and failure behavior are unspecified.
falsifier: Define one finite branch protocol that charges allocation only to resize and preserves all owners on allocation failure.

observable_contract: Resize allocates a new table, copies each FULL payload bitwise once to its destination, then swaps roots and frees old storage without dropping moved items.
reference_representation: Old and new sparse representations coexist until commit; only FULL slots are traversed as values.
live_state: Before commit old slots remain the semantic owners; after commit exactly the corresponding new slots are live.
owner_traffic: Each live payload relocates once; old allocation release performs no payload destruction.
provenance: All results re-root to the new allocation; old-root provenance becomes invalid.
failure_cleanup: If hashing aborts, the guard frees new raw storage without dropping copied bytes and old owners remain live.
fact_behavior: All old-root facts invalidate; new FULL and probe facts are produced only at successful commit.
c_route: C-4 resize composed with C-6 relocation/cleanup and C-7/C-11 re-rooting is not enumerated.
c0_dependency: C0-7 exact allocation and release row is unenumerated.
reference_events: One allocation, one hash per live item, one raw payload relocation per live item, one old-root release; no zero fill or second payload copy.
forced_delta: No extra structural event is identified; exact allocation and relocation authority are absent.
falsifier: Enumerate C0-7 and the relocation commit so generated structure requires exactly one allocation and one relocation per live item.

observable_contract: In-place rehash first maps every FULL control byte to DELETED while payload owners remain present.
reference_representation: One existing allocation is reused; DELETED temporarily denotes an unprocessed live payload, unlike ordinary vacant DELETED.
live_state: Every former FULL payload remains live but uses transition-specific authorization.
owner_traffic: No owner moves in the marking pass.
provenance: Ordinary DELETED grants no payload access; only a non-escapable rehash protocol may authorize these transition places.
failure_cleanup: Abort requires the rehash guard to distinguish processed and unprocessed owners.
fact_behavior: Ordinary occupancy facts invalidate; transition facts must not escape or be confused with normal DELETED state.
c_route: C-4 plus C-6 need a phase-specific sparse relation not enumerated by the frozen family.
reference_events: One control scan and tag rewrite per control byte as in the reference; no allocation, payload initialization, or atomic event.
forced_delta: No extra structural delta is identified; the phase-specific interpretation and cleanup are absent.
falsifier: Define a closed rehash phase whose DELETED-live state is inaccessible to ordinary sparse operations and needs no persistent extra tag.

observable_contract: An in-place item either restores FULL in its probe group, moves to an EMPTY slot, or swaps through a DELETED chain until placed.
reference_representation: Transition-local DELETED slots hold unprocessed live owners; EMPTY slots are vacant.
live_state: Each iteration preserves exactly one displaced owner and one live copy of every other item.
owner_traffic: Same-group restores without movement; EMPTY branch relocates once; DELETED branch swaps and continues with the displaced owner.
provenance: No intermediate bucket reference escapes; successful items receive fresh same-root occupancy facts.
failure_cleanup: Normal completion leaves only ordinary FULL, EMPTY, and permitted tombstone states.
fact_behavior: Each branch consumes and produces explicit transition facts; final commit invalidates the rehash phase.
c_route: C-4/C-6 relocation and swap protocols plus C-11 facts are named but their exact composition is absent.
reference_events: Reference probe, optional raw move or swap, and control updates only; no allocation, zeroing, or extra metadata.
forced_delta: No forced structural delta is identified; loop invariant and exact-use token are unspecified.
falsifier: Enumerate a finite invariant proving each live payload relocates at most once to its final place or participates in accounted swaps without duplication.

observable_contract: If the hash callback aborts during in-place rehash, the guard destroys every still-unprocessed live payload exactly once and leaves processed entries valid.
reference_representation: The same root contains a prefix-like processed set encoded by restored FULL and an unprocessed set encoded by transition-local DELETED.
live_state: Processed FULL payloads survive; each remaining transition-DELETED payload becomes dead after destruction.
owner_traffic: Every unprocessed owner is destroyed once; processed owners remain in the table.
provenance: No result borrow survives abort; surviving table provenance is fresh for the partial state.
failure_cleanup: The documented partial-progress state loses the unprocessed contents but must remain destructible and searchable for survivors.
fact_behavior: Abort invalidates all phase and pre-rehash facts; survivor facts must be re-established.
c_route: C-4/C-6 recoverable partial-progress cleanup and C-11 survivor facts are not enumerated.
reference_events: One cleanup scan plus one destruction per unprocessed item as in the reference guard; no allocation or owner duplication.
forced_delta: No forced structural delta is identified; exact partial-progress cleanup authority is absent.
falsifier: Define the abort state and prove each DELETED-live payload is destroyed once while no restored FULL payload is destroyed.

observable_contract: Successful rehash publishes new occupancy, probe, and growth facts only after all live owners reach valid FULL slots.
reference_representation: Same-root in-place rehash changes versions; resize also changes the physical root.
live_state: Every live payload has exactly one final FULL slot.
owner_traffic: No owner traffic occurs after the final placement except release of raw old storage on resize.
provenance: Old entries and references are invalid; new access derives from the final root and version.
failure_cleanup: No cleanup remains after successful commit.
fact_behavior: Pre-rehash facts invalidate; final control/counter observations may produce only enumerated replacement facts.
c_route: C-4/C-7 need invalidation and C-11 needs sparse rehash fact schemas; neither is enumerated.
c0_dependency: None beyond C0-7 for resize and C0-10 for group operations.
reference_events: Logical commit only; no extra runtime field or event is inherent.
forced_delta: No forced structural delta is identified; closed fact schemas and invalidators are absent.
falsifier: Enumerate final occupancy/probe/growth facts and demonstrate that no old-root or old-version fact can be consumed.
